title: acaulimalva crenata
intro: Acaulimalva crenata is a plant species that belongs to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as a type of hollyhock or a member of the mallow family. Acaulimalva crenata is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various health issues, where it exhibits medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Acaulimalva crenata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate gastrointestinal problems, and treat skin conditions, among others. Acaulimalva crenata can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused in oil for topical application, and it is also sometimes used as a poultice for wound healing.
